name: Belize
excerpt: Belize was the site of several Mayan city states until their decline at the end of the first millennium A.D. The British and Spanish disputed the region in the 17th and 18th centuries; it formally became the colony of British Honduras in 1862.

quick_facts.government: parliamentary democracy (National Assembly) under a constitutional monarchy; a Commonwealth realm
quick_facts.religions: Roman Catholic 40.1%, Protestant 31.5% (includes Pentecostal 8.4%, Seventh Day Adventist 5.4%, Anglican 4.7%, Mennonite 3.7%, Baptist 3.6%, Methodist 2.9%, Nazarene 2.8%), Jehovah's Witness 1.7%, other 10.5% (includes Baha'i, Buddhist, Hindu, Church of Jesus Christ, Muslim, Rastafarian, Salvation Army), unspecified 0.6%, none 15.5% (2010 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Mestizo 52.9%, Creole 25.9%, Maya 11.3%, Garifuna 6.1%, East Indian 3.9%, Mennonite 3.6%, White 1.2%, Asian 1%, other 1.2%, unknown 0.3% (2010 est.)
quick_facts.climate: tropical; very hot and humid; rainy season (May to November); dry season (February to May)
quick_facts.terrain: flat, swampy coastal plain; low mountains in south
quick_facts.natural_resources: arable land potential, timber, fish, hydropower
quick_facts.major_exports: raw sugar, bananas, fish, shellfish, refined petroleum (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: only country in Central America without a coastline on the North Pacific Ocean
summaries.history_summary: Belize was the site of several Mayan city states until their decline at the end of the first millennium A.D. The British and Spanish disputed the region in the 17th and 18th centuries; it formally became the colony of British Honduras in 1862. Territorial disputes between the UK and Guatemala delayed the independence of Belize until 1981. Guatemala refused to recognize the new nation until 1992, and the two countries are still involved in an ongoing border dispute. Tourism has become the mainstay of the economy. Current concerns include the country's heavy foreign debt burden, high crime rates, high unemployment combined with a majority youth population, growing involvement in the Mexican and South American drug trade, and one of the highest HIV/AIDS prevalence rates in Central America.
summaries.government_summary: parliamentary democracy (National Assembly) under a constitutional monarchy; a Commonwealth realm. Independence: 21 September 1981 (from the UK)
summaries.economy_summary: tourism- and agriculture-driven economy; strong post-pandemic rebound; innovative and ecological bond restructuring that significantly lowered public debt and expanded marine protections; central bank offering USD-denominated treasury notes; high mobility across borders

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Belize has a substantial Protestant population, including an existing Baptist witness, alongside its Catholic plurality. Pray for these evangelical churches to be strengthened and for Catholics to encounter the clear gospel.
prayer_points: Pray for Belize's existing Baptist and Protestant churches to be strengthened in sound biblical doctrine.
Pray for pastors to be trained in expository, Scripture-centered preaching.
Pray for Catholics practicing inherited religious tradition to encounter genuine salvation by grace through faith in Christ.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: Belize's substantial Protestant population, including an existing Baptist presence, is real ground to build on. Ongoing needs include strengthening biblical literacy among existing churches and reaching Belize's Catholic population with the clear gospel distinct from inherited religious tradition.
